patterns.name: empty_array_input
patterns.description: Function receives empty array where non-empty expected

self_test.source_file: export function sum(arr: number[]): number { return arr.reduce((a,b)=>a+b,0); }

patterns.name: null_argument
patterns.description: Function receives null for expected object argument

self_test.source_file: export function greet(name: string): string { return name ? `Hello ${name}` : 'Hello'; }

patterns.name: string_as_number
patterns.description: Function receives a string that looks like a number

self_test.source_file: export function double(n: number): number { return n * 2; }

patterns.name: promise_rejection
patterns.description: Function properly handles rejected promises without crashing

self_test.source_file: export async function fetchData(url: string): Promise<any> { const r = await fetch(url); return r.json(); }

patterns.name: loop_boundary
patterns.description: Function handles exactly the length of an array without accessing out of bounds

self_test.source_file: export function countPositive(arr: number[]): number { let c=0; for(let i=0;i<arr.length;i++){ if(arr[i]>0)c++; } return c; }
